page: Amazon Web Services m9g.24xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor. The instance features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor architecture includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 96 MB of L3 cache. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. No local storage or GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 191594.32 and a PassMark CPU score of 77010.83. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 13865.98 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 365.0 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the average tier at 1998.99. The server demonstrates top-tier capabilities in data processing, achieving 266.43 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 192.37 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

The ARM64 architecture offers high resource density and power efficiency, translating to qualitative cost advantages for multi-threaded workloads. However, single-threaded operations and encryption tasks perform in the average tier, representing a performance tradeoff. The instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference, where it processes 7B model prompts at 233.49 tokens per second.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.24xlarge is a general-purpose server utilizing the ARM64 architecture with an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz, 384.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, with no local storage or GPUs. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 191594.32 and a PassMark memory score of 4602.95. It excels in memory bandwidth and database operations, achieving 266.43 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. While single-core and encryption performance remain in the average tier, the high core density provides qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized tasks. This server is suited for multi-threaded workloads, web serving, database management, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 96 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores, 384 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.

tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose server featuring ninety-six dedicated physical cores and high-bandwidth memory for highly parallelized enterprise workloads.

384.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark results
Suited for Redis databases, web serving, and multi-threaded workloads
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
